questions.q: In which Indian state are the Udayagiri Caves of Kharavela located?
questions.explanation: The Udayagiri Caves lie near Bhubaneswar in the state of Odisha.

questions.q: The Udayagiri Caves were primarily carved as a hermitage for monks of which religion?
questions.explanation: The caves were excavated as a residential retreat for Jain ascetics.

questions.q: Near which Odishan city are the Udayagiri Caves situated?
questions.explanation: The caves are located just outside the capital city of Bhubaneswar.

questions.q: Udayagiri is usually paired with which neighbouring hill of caves?
questions.explanation: Udayagiri and the adjacent Khandagiri hill together form the famous twin cave complex.

questions.q: How were the Udayagiri Caves created?
questions.explanation: They are rock-cut caves excavated directly into the living sandstone hill.

questions.q: Which ancient king is most closely associated with patronising the Udayagiri Caves?
questions.explanation: King Kharavela of Kalinga is the ruler chiefly credited with the cave excavations.

questions.q: Kharavela ruled over which ancient region centred on present-day Odisha?
questions.explanation: Kharavela was the king of Kalinga, the historical region of coastal Odisha.

questions.q: The most famous cave at Udayagiri, whose name means 'Elephant Cave' and bears Kharavela's great inscription, is which 'gumpha'?
questions.explanation: Hathigumpha, the 'Elephant Cave,' is the most renowned cave because of its great inscription.

questions.q: The Udayagiri Caves are best described as which type of monument?
questions.explanation: They form an ancient rock-cut monastery of cells for Jain monks.

questions.q: The word 'Udayagiri' can be translated roughly as which of the following?
questions.explanation: Udayagiri means 'Sunrise Hill' or 'Hill of the Rising Sun.'

questions.q: To which dynasty did King Kharavela belong?
questions.explanation: Kharavela was a ruler of the Mahameghavahana dynasty of Kalinga.

questions.q: In roughly which period are the principal Udayagiri caves and the Hathigumpha inscription dated?
questions.explanation: Kharavela's reign and the cave excavations are generally placed in the 2nd-1st century BCE.

questions.q: In which script is the Hathigumpha inscription of Kharavela written?
questions.explanation: The Hathigumpha inscription is engraved in the Brahmi script.

questions.q: In which language is the Hathigumpha inscription composed?
questions.explanation: The inscription is written in a Prakrit language, recording Kharavela's deeds.

questions.q: The double-storeyed Rani Gumpha at Udayagiri is best known for its elaborate what?
questions.explanation: Rani Gumpha, the 'Queen's Cave,' is celebrated for its richly carved sculptural friezes.

questions.q: What was the main purpose of the small cells cut into the Udayagiri rock?
options: Sleeping and meditation quarters for monks
questions.explanation: The cramped cells served as living and meditation quarters for Jain ascetics.

questions.q: The Hathigumpha inscription provides a major source for the biography of which ruler?
questions.explanation: The inscription is the principal historical record of Kharavela's life and campaigns.

questions.q: The Tatowa Gumpha caves at Udayagiri take their name from carvings of which creatures over the doorways?
questions.explanation: Tatowa Gumpha means 'Parrot Cave,' named for the parrots carved on its arches.

questions.q: Which modern Indian body is responsible for protecting and maintaining the Udayagiri Caves?
options: Indian National Trust for Art only
questions.explanation: The Udayagiri and Khandagiri caves are a centrally protected monument under the Archaeological Survey of India.

questions.q: The rock into which the Udayagiri Caves were carved is principally which material?
questions.explanation: The caves are excavated into the natural sandstone of the hill.

questions.q: Which feature distinguishes Udayagiri's caves from many grand later rock-cut temples like Ellora?
options: They are mostly small, low residential cells
They are made of cast bronze
questions.explanation: Udayagiri's caves are modest monastic cells rather than monumental temple halls.

questions.q: Kharavela is remembered in tradition as a notable royal patron of which faith?
questions.explanation: Kharavela is celebrated as a great royal patron of Jainism, which the caves served.

questions.q: The Hathigumpha inscription is carved on the brow of an overhang directly above which cave?
questions.explanation: The inscription runs along the overhanging brow of Hathigumpha, the Elephant Cave.

questions.q: The Hathigumpha inscription is generally counted as having roughly how many lines of text?
questions.explanation: Kharavela's record consists of about seventeen lines of Brahmi text.

questions.q: Ananta Gumpha at the twin-hill complex is named for carvings of what?
questions.explanation: Ananta Gumpha takes its name from the serpents (nagas) sculpted at its entrance.

questions.q: According to the Hathigumpha inscription, before becoming king Kharavela held office as which rank?
questions.explanation: The inscription records that Kharavela served as crown prince before his coronation.

questions.q: Ganesha Gumpha at Udayagiri owes its modern name to which feature?
options: A later image of Ganesha carved in it
questions.explanation: The cave is named for a figure of the god Ganesha carved at the site in a later period.

questions.q: The Mahameghavahana rulers of Kharavela's line are sometimes also referred to as the kings of which name?
questions.explanation: Kharavela's dynasty is associated with the Chedi or Cheti royal line of Kalinga.

questions.q: Compared with Buddhist cave sites like Ajanta, the Udayagiri caves notably lack which feature?
questions.explanation: Unlike Ajanta, Udayagiri is famed for sculpture and inscription rather than surviving painted murals.

questions.q: On the neighbouring Khandagiri hill, a later shrine venerates images of which Jain figures?
questions.explanation: Khandagiri preserves later carvings and a temple devoted to the Jain Tirthankaras.
